title: Establish module foundation and shared types
purpose: Foundation module for the Aion integration surface -- shared types (ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, HistoryEvent), channel naming convention constructors, and the AionSurfaceError taxonomy. Every other brief in the cluster imports from this module. The types defined here are the vocabulary for dispatch conversations (ADR-007), typed channel validation (ADR-002), and the signal/history/worker subsystems. Nothing in this brief dispatches, delivers, streams, or registers -- it provides the shared language that those briefs build on.
task: Create the four foundation files for the Aion integration surface at crates/liminal/src/aion/. mod.rs is the module root with only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports (no logic, no type definitions, no function bodies -- constraint CN3). types.rs defines the shared types used across the cluster: ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, and HistoryEvent envelope. channels.rs provides deterministic channel naming constructors that enforce the aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id} convention (constraint CN7) and reject malformed inputs. error.rs defines AionSurfaceError with variants for dispatch, signal, streaming, and channel naming failures, implemented via thiserror. All public types implement Debug (constraint CN4). No file exceeds 500 lines (constraint CN1). This brief does NOT implement dispatch, signal delivery, history streaming, or worker registration -- those are AION-002 through AION-005.

requirements.title: Module root with pub mod declarations only
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/aion/mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations for types, channels, error, dispatch, codec, signal, history, and worker submodules, plus pub use re-exports of key types. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any function bodies, struct definitions, enum definitions, trait definitions, or impl blocks.
acceptance: mod.rs compiles and contains pub mod declarations for all submodules defined in design.json structure
grep -E '(fn |struct |enum |trait |impl )' mod.rs returns no matches
pub use re-exports make AionSurfaceError, ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, and HistoryEvent available as crate::aion::*

files: crates/liminal/src/aion/mod.rs:1 — current placeholder; replace with declarations/re-exports only.
docs/design/aion/design.json:32-42 — authoritative Aion module structure: types, channels, error, dispatch, codec, signal, history, worker.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs:1-15 — good module-root pattern: only `pub mod` declarations followed by `pub use` re-exports.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-12 — crate root currently omits `pub mod aion;`, so `crate::aion::*` acceptance is otherwise impossible.
docs/design/aion/briefs/AION-001.json:14-21 — R1 acceptance, including the grep constraint and required re-exported names.
context: `mod.rs` convention in this crate is declaration/re-export only; do not place inline module bodies, type definitions, functions, traits, or impls there.
Declaring `pub mod dispatch;`, `pub mod codec;`, `pub mod signal;`, `pub mod history;`, and `pub mod worker;` requires sibling files to exist; none currently exist under `crates/liminal/src/aion/`.

The required public surface includes at least `AionSurfaceError`, `ActivityRequest`, `ActivityResult`, `SignalPayload`, `WorkerCapacity`, and `HistoryEvent`; re-exporting `Payload`, `ChannelName`, and the naming constructors would be consistent but is beyond the explicit acceptance list.
`crate::aion::*` cannot resolve unless `crates/liminal/src/lib.rs` declares `pub mod aion;`, which is not listed in the brief’s file set.
scout.approach: Replace the placeholder `aion/mod.rs` with only `pub mod channels;`, `pub mod codec;`, `pub mod dispatch;`, `pub mod error;`, `pub mod history;`, `pub mod signal;`, `pub mod types;`, and `pub mod worker;`, followed by `pub use error::AionSurfaceError;` and `pub use types::{ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, HistoryEvent, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity}` (plus `Payload` if desired). To keep the crate compiling, create empty stub files for future modules or resolve the file-list conflict with the workflow owner; do not use inline `pub mod dispatch {}` blocks because CN3 says declarations only. Also account for the missing `pub mod aion;` in `lib.rs` if the acceptance criterion for `crate::aion::*` is enforced.
scout.notes: There are two acceptance/file-list mismatches that will cost time if ignored: future submodule declarations need files, and crate-level availability needs `lib.rs` exposure. If the orchestrator forbids edits outside the listed files, R1 cannot both compile and expose `crate::aion::*` in the current repository state.

A targeted grep for function/type/impl keywords in mod.rs produced no matches, and crate-root exposure makes the surface available through crate::aion::* as required.

requirements.title: Shared types for the integration surface
requirements.spec: types.rs SHALL define ActivityRequest (activity type, input payload, task queue name, timeout configuration), ActivityResult (output payload, error variant), SignalPayload (signal name, typed payload bytes, content-type tag), WorkerCapacity (max concurrent activities, activity type filter), and HistoryEvent (workflow event envelope with sequence number, event type, timestamp, and opaque payload). All types SHALL implement Debug and Clone. ActivityRequest and ActivityResult SHALL use the type-erased Payload model (bytes + content-type) consistent with Aion's opaque payload approach. The types SHALL NOT carry generic type parameters.
acceptance: ActivityRequest has fields: activity_type (String), input (Payload), task_queue (String), schedule_to_close_timeout (Option<Duration>), start_to_close_timeout (Option<Duration>)
ActivityResult is an enum with Completed { output: Payload } and Failed { error: AionSurfaceError } variants
SignalPayload has fields: signal_name (String), payload (Payload)
WorkerCapacity has fields: max_concurrent (usize), activity_types (Vec<String>)
HistoryEvent has fields: sequence (u64), event_type (String), timestamp (SystemTime or equivalent), payload (Payload)
All five types derive Debug and Clone
No type uses a generic parameter <T>

files: docs/design/aion/briefs/AION-001.json:30-50 — exact field list for ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, and HistoryEvent.
docs/design/aion/design.json:32-41 — places `types.rs` as the shared vocabulary for all later Aion modules.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:125-145 — local convention for public protocol types deriving `Clone`/`Debug` and using public fields.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-34 — compact public data type style with derives and explicit fields.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing `LiminalError` is `Debug`/`Error` but not `Clone`, which matters because `ActivityResult` must clone its error variant.
context: Use `std::time::{Duration, SystemTime}` for timeout and timestamp fields; no extra dependency is needed.
All five public types must derive at least `Debug` and `Clone`, and none should have generic parameters such as `ActivityRequest<T>`.

`ActivityResult::Failed { error: AionSurfaceError }` means `AionSurfaceError` must also be cloneable if `ActivityResult` derives `Clone`. Avoid embedding non-Clone source errors directly in `AionSurfaceError`.
This brief defines passive vocabulary only: no dispatch calls, channel publishing, validation, recorder hooks, or worker capacity tracking.
scout.approach: Implement `types.rs` as a small public data module: define `Payload` first (see R5), then `ActivityRequest` with the two `Option<Duration>` timeout fields, `ActivityResult` with `Completed { output: Payload }` and `Failed { error: AionSurfaceError }`, `SignalPayload`, `WorkerCapacity`, and `HistoryEvent` with `timestamp: SystemTime`. Use public fields matching the acceptance names exactly, import `super::error::AionSurfaceError`, and derive `Clone, Debug` everywhere; add `PartialEq`/`Eq` only if it does not force awkward error equality choices.
scout.notes: The clone requirement couples R2 to R4: if `AionSurfaceError` stores `LiminalError`, `std::io::Error`, or `Box<dyn Error>` as a source, the derive on `ActivityResult` will fail or violate the brief. Prefer cloneable context fields or a local cloneable typed source-detail enum if source chaining is needed.

files_changed.note: Defined Payload, ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, and HistoryEvent.

dev.how: types.rs defines the required passive vocabulary with exact field names and type-erased Payload usage: ActivityRequest has activity_type, input, task_queue, and the two Option<Duration> timeouts; ActivityResult has Completed { output: Payload } and Failed { error: AionSurfaceError }; SignalPayload, WorkerCapacity, and HistoryEvent match the brief. All five required types derive Clone and Debug and have no generic type parameters.

checklist.note: The public Aion types derive Debug; AionSurfaceError implements std::error::Error via thiserror.
Targeted wc -l over crates/liminal/src/aion/*.rs showed all Aion files under 500 lines.

requirements.title: Channel naming convention constructors
requirements.spec: channels.rs SHALL provide constructor functions that produce deterministic channel names following the aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id} convention: dispatch_channel(namespace, task_queue) returning aion.dispatch.{namespace}.{task_queue}, signal_channel(namespace, workflow_id) returning aion.signal.{namespace}.{workflow_id}, and history_channel(namespace, workflow_id) returning aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id}. WHEN a namespace or id argument contains a dot (.) or is empty, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Err(AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName) with a message identifying the invalid input. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow ad-hoc string construction of channel names outside this module.
acceptance: dispatch_channel("default", "email-queue") returns Ok("aion.dispatch.default.email-queue")
signal_channel("prod", "wf-123") returns Ok("aion.signal.prod.wf-123")
history_channel("prod", "wf-123") returns Ok("aion.history.prod.wf-123")
dispatch_channel("bad.ns", "q") returns Err(AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName { .. })
signal_channel("", "wf-1") returns Err(AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName { .. })
history_channel("ns", "") returns Err(AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName { .. })
The return type is a newtype (e.g., ChannelName) that prevents construction outside channels.rs

files: docs/design/aion/briefs/AION-001.json:52-70 — exact constructor names, valid examples, invalid examples, and newtype requirement.
docs/design/aion/design.json:6 — solution text defines the `aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id}` convention and bans ad-hoc construction.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-25 — core channel configuration still stores a `String`; Aion’s newtype should guard construction at the integration surface boundary.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:271-299 — existing Result-returning constructor pattern that validates invariants before constructing values.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:453-466 — representative tests using `matches!` for constructor validation failures.
context: Return `Result<ChannelName, AionSurfaceError>` from `dispatch_channel`, `signal_channel`, and `history_channel`; the newtype should wrap a private `String` field.
Validation is intentionally narrow: reject empty strings and any dot (`.`) in namespace or id/task queue/workflow id; hyphens like `email-queue` are accepted by the examples.
Expose safe observation/conversion such as `as_str()`, `Display`, and perhaps `From<ChannelName> for String`, but do not expose `From<String>` or a public unchecked `new` constructor.
`channels.rs` may use `format!` internally; the “no ad-hoc string construction” rule is about keeping external callers from creating arbitrary Aion channel names.
scout.approach: Create `channels.rs` with `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)] pub struct ChannelName(String);`, a private helper to validate one part and another helper to assemble `aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id}`. Each public constructor should call the helper with the correct kind (`dispatch`, `signal`, `history`) and return `AionSurfaceError::InvalidChannelName { .. }` with the invalid input and a reason on failure. Add focused unit tests for the three accepted strings and the three rejected examples, comparing via `name.as_str()` or `name.to_string()` because the public return type is a newtype, not a raw `String`.
scout.notes: Do not over-validate names with an identifier regex unless the design changes; the brief only bans dots and empties. Also avoid placing `ChannelName` construction helpers in `mod.rs` or another module, or the private-field guarantee becomes meaningless.

files_changed.note: Added private-field ChannelName newtype plus dispatch, signal, and history channel constructors.

dev.how: channels.rs now centralizes Aion channel-name construction. dispatch_channel, signal_channel, and history_channel return Result<ChannelName, AionSurfaceError>, build the required aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id} strings internally, reject empty or dotted namespace/id inputs with InvalidChannelName context, and expose only safe observation/conversion APIs such as as_str, Display, and From<ChannelName> for String.

checklist.note: Constructor tests cover the three accepted examples and the three specified malformed inputs, all returning the expected names/errors.

stories.note: Validated deterministic channel names establish the dispatch/signal/history routing surface workflow authors can use transparently.

acceptance.evidence: Empty id rejection is implemented at crates/liminal/src/aion/channels.rs:68-80; unit test `aion::channels::tests::rejects_empty_workflow_id` at lines 148-155 passed in `cargo test aion`.

acceptance.criterion: The return type is a newtype (e.g., ChannelName) that prevents construction outside channels.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/channels.rs:5-7 defines `pub struct ChannelName(String)` with a private tuple field; constructors return `Result<ChannelName, AionSurfaceError>` at lines 34-60. Final grep for ad-hoc Aion channel construction outside channels.rs produced no output.

requirements.title: AionSurfaceError taxonomy
requirements.spec: error.rs SHALL define AionSurfaceError as a thiserror enum with at minimum the following variants: DispatchFailed (worker selection or conversation failure), WorkerCrashed (process link exit detected), SignalDeliveryFailed (publish to signal channel failed), SignalValidationFailed (payload does not match declared type), StreamingFailed (history channel publish or subscribe failure), InvalidChannelName (malformed namespace or id input), and ChannelLifecycleError (creation or teardown failure). Each variant SHALL carry enough context (source error, channel name, workflow id) to diagnose the failure without additional logging. AionSurfaceError SHALL implement std::error::Error via thiserror and Debug.
acceptance: AionSurfaceError has at least 7 variants: DispatchFailed, WorkerCrashed, SignalDeliveryFailed, SignalValidationFailed, StreamingFailed, InvalidChannelName, ChannelLifecycleError
Each variant's Display output includes the channel name or workflow id relevant to the failure
AionSurfaceError implements std::error::Error (verified by std::error::Error trait bound compilation)
AionSurfaceError implements Debug
No variant uses #[from] on a generic or boxed error -- source errors are typed

files: docs/design/aion/briefs/AION-001.json:72-89 — required AionSurfaceError variants, Display/context expectations, and ban on generic/boxed `#[from]` sources.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing crate-level `thiserror` style with variant-specific display messages.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:3-48 — richer `thiserror` enum deriving `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq` with explicit variant fields.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-14 — `thiserror = "2"` is already available in the liminal crate.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:41-70 — channel APIs return `LiminalError`; importing those directly into Aion errors would conflict with `Clone` unless handled carefully.
context: `AionSurfaceError` should derive `Debug` and `thiserror::Error`; deriving `Clone` is also practically required by `ActivityResult` in R2.
Every required variant’s `#[error(...)]` text should mention the relevant channel name, workflow id, worker id, signal name, or invalid input so failures are diagnosable without extra logs.
The brief forbids generic or boxed `#[from]` sources; it does not require broad conversion impls in this foundation brief.
If a field is named `source`, `thiserror` treats it as an error source; only use that name for a cloneable concrete type that implements `Error`.
scout.approach: Create `error.rs` with `#[derive(Clone, Debug, thiserror::Error)] pub enum AionSurfaceError` and at least `DispatchFailed`, `WorkerCrashed`, `SignalDeliveryFailed`, `SignalValidationFailed`, `StreamingFailed`, `InvalidChannelName`, and `ChannelLifecycleError`. Use explicit context fields such as `channel_name: String`, `workflow_id: String`, `worker_id: String`, `signal_name: String`, `input: String`, and `message: String`, and write `#[error("...")]` strings that include those fields. If preserving source chaining now is important, add a small cloneable typed source-detail enum in the same file; otherwise keep message/context fields and add conversion helpers in later behavior briefs when concrete source APIs exist.
scout.notes: The tempting implementation `#[source] source: LiminalError` makes `AionSurfaceError: Clone` fail because `LiminalError` currently does not derive `Clone`. Avoid `Box<dyn std::error::Error>` too: it violates the brief and also blocks Clone.

files_changed.note: Defined cloneable Debug thiserror AionSurfaceError taxonomy.

dev.how: AionSurfaceError is a thiserror enum deriving Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, and Error, with the required DispatchFailed, WorkerCrashed, SignalDeliveryFailed, SignalValidationFailed, StreamingFailed, InvalidChannelName, and ChannelLifecycleError variants. Each variant carries explicit cloneable diagnostic context such as channel_name, workflow_id, worker_id, signal_name, input, part, and message, and no variant uses boxed/generic sources or #[from].

checklist.note: The required dispatch, worker, signal, streaming, invalid-name, and lifecycle variants are present.
AionSurfaceError implements std::error::Error through thiserror and derives Debug.

acceptance.criterion: AionSurfaceError has at least 7 variants: DispatchFailed, WorkerCrashed, SignalDeliveryFailed, SignalValidationFailed, StreamingFailed, InvalidChannelName, ChannelLifecycleError
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:3-91 defines AionSurfaceError with all seven required variants: DispatchFailed (6-13), WorkerCrashed (19-28), SignalDeliveryFailed (34-43), SignalValidationFailed (49-58), StreamingFailed (64-71), InvalidChannelName (75-82), and ChannelLifecycleError (86-91).

acceptance.criterion: Each variant's Display output includes the channel name or workflow id relevant to the failure
acceptance.evidence: Display templates include workflow/channel context at crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:5, 16-18, 31-33, 46-48, 61-63, 74, and 85. Hardened tests `dispatch_display_includes_channel_and_workflow_context`, `worker_crash_display_includes_channel_workflow_and_worker_context`, `signal_display_includes_channel_workflow_and_signal_context`, `streaming_display_includes_channel_and_workflow_context`, and `invalid_channel_name_display_identifies_invalid_input` at lines 119-210 passed in `cargo test aion`.

acceptance.criterion: AionSurfaceError implements std::error::Error (verified by std::error::Error trait bound compilation)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:2 derives `thiserror::Error`; hardened test `aion::error::tests::implements_std_error_and_debug` at lines 101-116 compiles an Error trait bound and passed in `cargo test aion`.

acceptance.criterion: AionSurfaceError implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:2 derives Debug; hardened test `aion::error::tests::implements_std_error_and_debug` formats the error with `{:?}` at line 110 and passed in `cargo test aion`.

acceptance.criterion: No variant uses #[from] on a generic or boxed error -- source errors are typed
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:3-91 has no `#[from]`, boxed, or generic error fields. Final grep `grep -rnE '#\[from\]|Box<|dyn Error' crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs || true` produced no output.

issues: The dev/brief verification claimed unit tests covered error Display output, but the actual pre-review Aion tests only covered channel naming and Payload::default; no AionSurfaceError Display or std::error::Error trait-bound test existed.
fixes: Added AionSurfaceError tests in crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs:94-212 covering std::error::Error + Debug, Display context for dispatch/worker/signal/streaming errors, and invalid channel-name Display context. The tests use validated channel constructors so no ad-hoc Aion channel strings are introduced outside channels.rs.

requirements.title: Payload type for type-erased data
requirements.spec: types.rs SHALL define a Payload struct carrying raw bytes (Vec<u8>) and a content-type tag (String) for type-erased data exchange. Payload SHALL implement Debug, Clone, and Default. The Default implementation SHALL produce an empty payload with an empty content-type. Payload is the single data-carrying type used by ActivityRequest, ActivityResult, SignalPayload, and HistoryEvent -- no type uses a separate bytes field.
acceptance: Payload has fields: data (Vec<u8>), content_type (String)
Payload::default() returns Payload { data: vec![], content_type: String::new() }
ActivityRequest.input, ActivityResult::Completed.output, SignalPayload.payload, and HistoryEvent.payload all use the Payload type
Payload derives Debug and Clone

files: docs/design/aion/briefs/AION-001.json:91-107 — exact `Payload` fields, default behavior, and required consumers.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:189-195,216-222 — existing protocol frames treat message bodies as opaque `Vec<u8>` payloads.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/payload.rs:27-34 — internal protocol helpers distinguish raw bytes from strings; do not reuse these private helpers as the public Aion payload model.
docs/design/aion/design.json:17-22 — Aion goals require dispatch, signal, and history operations to share one recorder-compatible vocabulary.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-25 — typed channel config carries schema metadata separately; Aion `Payload` should carry only bytes plus content type.
context: `Payload` is the single type-erased data carrier for this brief: raw `Vec<u8>` plus a `String` content-type tag.
Derived `Default` on `Vec<u8>` and `String` exactly satisfies the required empty payload with empty content type.
Activity input/output, signal payload, and history event payload must use `Payload`; do not add parallel `bytes`, `data`, or `content_type` fields to those outer types.
ADR-002 typed validation belongs in later codec/signal/channel publish paths, not in generic Rust parameters on `Payload` or the request/result types.
scout.approach: Implement `Payload` near the top of `types.rs` as `#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)] pub struct Payload { pub data: Vec<u8>, pub content_type: String }` and then thread that type through `ActivityRequest.input`, `ActivityResult::Completed.output`, `SignalPayload.payload`, and `HistoryEvent.payload`. Keep it passive: no serialization, schema lookup, channel publish, or typed validation behavior. Add a simple test or assertion that `Payload::default()` has empty `data` and `content_type`.
scout.notes: R5 should be implemented in the same edit as R2 to avoid churn in `types.rs`. If adding a convenience constructor, mark it `#[must_use]`, but no constructor is required and a no-impl struct is less likely to trip clippy or blur the “types only” boundary.

files_changed.note: Added Payload as the single type-erased data carrier and threaded it through request/result/signal/history types.

dev.how: Payload is defined with public data: Vec<u8> and content_type: String fields, deriving Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, and Eq. Derived Default produces an empty byte vector and empty content type, and ActivityRequest.input, ActivityResult::Completed.output, SignalPayload.payload, and HistoryEvent.payload all use Payload rather than separate byte/content fields.

checklist.note: Payload and all other public Aion data types derive Debug; AionSurfaceError implements Error via thiserror.

stories.note: The common opaque Payload model keeps activity dispatch, signals, and history aligned with the same transparent type-erased surface.

acceptance.criterion: Payload has fields: data (Vec<u8>), content_type (String)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:5-12 defines Payload with public data: Vec<u8> and content_type: String fields.

acceptance.criterion: Payload::default() returns Payload { data: vec![], content_type: String::new() }
acceptance.evidence: Payload derives Default at crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:6; unit test `aion::types::tests::payload_default_is_empty` at lines 73-79 passed in `cargo test aion`.

acceptance.criterion: ActivityRequest.input, ActivityResult::Completed.output, SignalPayload.payload, and HistoryEvent.payload all use the Payload type
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:20 uses Payload for ActivityRequest.input, line 33 for ActivityResult::Completed.output, line 44 for SignalPayload.payload, and line 66 for HistoryEvent.payload.

acceptance.criterion: Payload derives Debug and Clone
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:6 derives Clone and Debug for Payload.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement activity dispatch logic -- that is AION-002.
SHALL NOT implement signal delivery or validation logic -- that is AION-003.
SHALL NOT implement history event streaming -- that is AION-004.
SHALL NOT implement worker registration or capacity tracking -- that is AION-005.
SHALL NOT import from Aion engine internals (aion crate) -- the integration surface is a consumer of public APIs only (principle P1).
SHALL NOT import from liminal core internals -- consume the public channel and conversation APIs only.
SHALL NOT define any behavior or side effects -- this brief provides types, constructors, and errors only.
verification: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean with the new files.
cargo test for the aion module passes (unit tests for channel naming validation and error Display output).
No file in crates/liminal/src/aion/ exceeds 500 lines (wc -l).
grep -rn 'use aion::' crates/liminal/src/aion/ returns no references to Aion engine internals.
All public types implement Debug (verified by #[derive(Debug)] or manual impl presence).
